CLARITY.justification: The paper is overall well-structured (Intro → Related Work → Theory → Algorithm → Experiments → Discussion → Conclusion) and the high-level contributions (batch active learning for deep nets using a Fisher/KFAC-based variational criterion and a greedy selection) are described early. However the notation and technical presentation are uneven: several symbols are not fully defined (the ψ, τ Kronecker-block contents are explicitly omitted in §3.2), approximations and assumptions are introduced with little justification or consistent notation (observed vs expected Fisher, many approximations around Eqs. 10–18). Presentation artifacts reduce clarity: figures/tables are repeatedly referenced (e.g. table 1/2, figs. 1–3) but not shown here, and the text contains duplicated sentences and grammatical/formatting issues, which together make some sections hard to follow.

ORIGINALITY.justification: The paper presents a notable novel combination: using variational free energy with an MLE-derived asymptotic Gaussian posterior together with Kronecker-factored (KFAC) approximations of the Fisher to produce a backpropagation-free, scalable batch active-learning criterion for deep CNNs. It explicitly differentiates from Graves (2011) by avoiding per-query variational updates and from submodular/KL batch-selection works (Wei et al., Zhang & Oles) by adapting Fisher/KFAC approximations to deep networks. While the core ingredients (variational inference, MLE asymptotics, KFAC, greedy batch selection) are existing, their integration to yield a practical, scalable batch active-learning method for deep networks is a non-trivial and sufficiently distinct contribution that would interest experts.

SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper's main claim (a scalable variational/Fisher-based batch AL for deep nets) is only partially supported: it relies on strong asymptotic MLE/Gaussian assumptions in small-label regimes (Sec.3.1) and several ad-hoc approximations whose validity is not justified. There are concrete mathematical issues (Eq.11 in Sec.3.3 appears to use I^{-1}_A where the Hessian/Fisher itself should appear; multiple steps replace trace-of-inverse by inverse-of-trace without justification in Secs.3.3–3.4). The greedy/additivity approximation for quadratic terms in Sec.4 neglects cross-terms and is not shown to be accurate. Experiments (Sec.5) are limited to MNIST/USPS with weak baselines, no reported variances/statistical tests, and missing ablations for key approximations, so conclusions are not fully supported.
